criteria: LPUSH prepends elements to list head in O(1) time using doubly-linked list structure
RPUSH appends elements to list tail in O(1) time using doubly-linked list structure
LPOP removes and returns element from list head in O(1)
RPOP removes and returns element from list tail in O(1)
LRANGE supports negative indices (-1 = last element) with correct bounds calculation
LRANGE optimizes traversal by starting from closer end (head or tail)
SADD adds unique members to set returning count of newly added elements (not total size)
SISMEMBER returns 1 if member exists, 0 if not found or key missing
SMEMBERS returns all set members as array in O(n) time
HSET stores field-value pairs in hash, returns count of fields set
HGET retrieves field value in O(1), returns null if field missing
HGETALL returns alternating field-value pairs in O(n) time
WRONGTYPE error returned for any type mismatch without modifying existing data
Type checking occurs BEFORE any data modification in command handlers
List implemented as doubly-linked structure (not array/vector) for O(1) head/tail operations
Set implemented as hash table for O(1) membership testing
Hash implemented as nested hash table for O(1) field access
All keys have type tag stored in RedisObject structure
All data types are binary-safe using SDS strings
SAVE command blocks event loop and serializes all keys, values, types, and unexpired TTLs to custom binary RDB format
BGSAVE forks child process that writes snapshot while parent continues serving clients without blocking
Fork-based BGSAVE uses OS copy-on-write: pages are shared read-only until parent modifies them, enabling lock-free reading of parent memory
RDB file is written to temporary file, fsynced, then atomically renamed to prevent corruption from incomplete writes
On startup, server loads RDB file and reconstructs all keys, values, data types, and unexpired TTLs
Automatic save triggers fire when configurable thresholds are met (e.g., 'save 900 1' triggers after 900s if >=1 change)
Expired keys are NOT written to RDB file - serialization skips keys with expired timestamps
BGSAVE returns error if already in progress to prevent concurrent background saves
RDB format uses variable-length encoding for lengths to minimize file size
RDB header contains magic string ('REDIS0011') and version for validation
Every write command is appended to the AOF file in RESP wire format
AOF replay on startup reconstructs database state by re-executing all logged commands
fsync policy 'always' fsyncs after every write, providing strongest durability but lowest throughput
fsync policy 'everysec' fsyncs once per second, providing bounded 1-second data loss and practical default
fsync policy 'no' delegates durability to the OS, providing highest throughput but up to 30s data loss
BGREWRITEAOF rewrites the AOF to compact equivalent using forked child process without blocking event loop
During BGREWRITEAOF, new write commands are appended to both old AOF and rewrite buffer
After BGREWRITEAOF completes, rewrite buffer contents are appended to new AOF before switching
BGSAVE and BGREWRITEAOF are mutually exclusive - if one is running, the other is queued
On startup, if both AOF and RDB exist and AOF is enabled, AOF takes priority for recovery
Automatic rewrite triggers when AOF grows beyond configured threshold (e.g., 100% of base size)
Expired keys are not written during AOF rewrite (same as RDB)
AOF write happens after command executes successfully, not before
Read commands (GET, LRANGE, etc.) are not logged to AOF - only state-modifying commands
AOF file corruption is detected and handled appropriately during replay
SUBSCRIBE registers client on one or more channels and client receives subscription confirmation array per channel
PUBLISH sends message to all channel subscribers and all pattern subscribers that match, returning receiver count
UNSUBSCRIBE removes client from specified channels or all channels if no argument provided
PUNSUBSCRIBE removes client from specified patterns or all patterns if no argument provided
PSUBSCRIBE matches channels using glob syntax (* matches any chars, ? matches single char, [] matches set/range)
Subscribed client can only issue SUBSCRIBE, PSUBSCRIBE, UNSUBSCRIBE, PUNSUBSCRIBE, and PING commands—other commands return ERR
Client disconnection automatically removes all channel and pattern subscriptions without leaking memory
Pattern matching uses glob syntax (*, ?, []) not substring matching—news.* matches news.sports but not newssports
Output buffer limits disconnect slow subscribers without blocking fast subscribers during PUBLISH
PUBSUB CHANNELS [pattern] returns list of active channels, optionally filtered by pattern
Every disconnect path (QUIT, network error, timeout) calls pubsub_cleanup_client to prevent memory leaks

criteria.description: Event loop uses epoll_wait() to multiplex I/O across all connected clients in a single thread
criteria.verification: Verify code uses epoll_wait() as the primary I/O multiplexing mechanism

criteria.description: No threads are used for client handling—all I/O is non-blocking and driven by the event loop
criteria.verification: Search for pthread_create or similar; verify all client operations are non-blocking (O_NONBLOCK)

criteria.description: Server handles at least 100 concurrent clients without degradation
criteria.verification: Connect 100 clients, each send 10 commands, verify all complete successfully

criteria.description: Throughput is at least 10,000 PING/PONG operations per second with 10 concurrent clients
criteria.verification: Run benchmark, measure operations/second, verify >= 10000

criteria.description: Event loop integrates time-based callbacks for future use by expiration and background tasks
criteria.verification: Verify time_event system implemented with callback mechanism

criteria.description: EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK from non-blocking sockets handled correctly without data loss
criteria.verification: Review client_read/client_write for proper error handling of these errors

criteria.description: Edge-triggered epoll events are properly re-armed after processing
criteria.verification: Verify all available data is consumed before returning from read handler

criteria.requirement: SET stores key-value pair; subsequent GET returns stored value
criteria.test_method: Execute SET foo bar, then GET foo, verify returns bar

criteria.requirement: GET returns null bulk string for non-existent keys
DEL removes keys and returns count
criteria.test_method: SET a 1, SET b 2, DEL a b, verify returns 2

criteria.requirement: SET supports NX flag (set only if not exists)
criteria.test_method: SET foo 1, SET foo 2 NX, verify foo still 1

criteria.requirement: SET supports XX flag (set only if exists)
criteria.test_method: SET foo 1, SET bar 2 XX, verify foo unchanged
SET foo 1 EX 10, verify stored with TTL
SET n 10, INCR n, verify 11, DECR n, verify 10

criteria.requirement: INCR returns error for non-integer values
criteria.test_method: SET s hello, INCR s, verify error
MSET a 1 b 2, MGET a b c, verify array with 1,2,null
SET \u0001\u0000\u0002 value, GET \u0001\u0000\u0002, verify value returned
SET s string, LPUSH s item, verify WRONGTYPE error

criteria.requirement: O(1) hash table operations verified by benchmark
criteria.test_method: Insert 100k keys, time 100k lookups, verify <10ms total

criteria.description: Doubly-linked list implements O(1) LPUSH/RPUSH
criteria.verification: Add 100k elements, measure throughput >50k ops/sec

criteria.description: Doubly-linked list implements O(1) LPOP/RPOP
criteria.verification: Remove 100k elements, measure throughput >50k ops/sec

criteria.description: LRANGE supports negative indices correctly
criteria.verification: LRANGE list -3 -1 returns correct last 3 elements

criteria.description: LRANGE optimizes by traversing from closer end
criteria.verification: LRANGE list -100 -1 starts from tail, not head

criteria.description: Hash set implements O(1) SADD
criteria.verification: Add 100k elements, verify O(1) average

criteria.description: Hash set implements O(1) SISMEMBER
criteria.verification: Query 100k elements, verify O(1) average

criteria.description: SADD returns count of newly added elements
criteria.verification: SADD set a b a c returns 3 (a added once)

criteria.description: SMEMBERS returns all elements
criteria.verification: SMEMBERS on set with 1000 elements returns array of 1000

criteria.description: Nested hash table implements O(1) HSET
criteria.verification: Add 100k field-value pairs, verify O(1) average

criteria.description: Nested hash table implements O(1) HGET
criteria.verification: Query 100k fields, verify O(1) average

criteria.description: HGETALL returns alternating field-value array
criteria.verification: HGETALL returns [field1,val1,field2,val2,...]

criteria.description: HINCRBY handles numeric increment
criteria.verification: HSET f 10; HINCRBY f 5 returns 15

criteria.description: WRONGTYPE error returned for type mismatch
criteria.verification: LPUSH on string key returns WRONGTYPE error

criteria.description: WRONGTYPE does not modify existing data
criteria.verification: SET key val; LPUSH key item; GET key returns original val

criteria.description: All list commands check type
criteria.verification: LPUSH on set key returns WRONGTYPE

criteria.description: All set commands check type
criteria.verification: SADD on list key returns WRONGTYPE

criteria.description: All hash commands check type
criteria.verification: HSET on string key returns WRONGTYPE

criteria.description: List supports binary-safe elements
criteria.verification: LPUSH with \u0000\u0001\u0002 elements preserves exact bytes

criteria.description: Set supports binary-safe members
criteria.verification: SADD with embedded null bytes works correctly

criteria.description: Hash supports binary-safe fields and values
criteria.verification: HSET with null bytes in field/value works correctly

criteria.description: LPOP on empty list returns null
criteria.verification: LPOP on non-existent key returns null bulk string

criteria.description: HGET on missing field returns null
criteria.verification: HGET nonexistent returns null bulk string

criteria.description: LRANGE on empty range returns empty array
criteria.verification: LRANGE on start > stop returns empty array

criteria.description: SMEMBERS on empty set returns empty array
criteria.verification: SMEMBERS on non-existent key returns empty array

criteria.description: HGETALL on empty hash returns empty array
criteria.verification: HGETALL on non-existent key returns empty array

criteria: rdbSave() blocks event loop and writes all unexpired keys to RDB file
rdbSaveBackground() forks child process without blocking parent
fork() child uses copy-on-write semantics to read parent memory
expired keys are NOT written to RDB file (skip during iteration)
RDB file written to temp file and atomically renamed for crash safety
rdbLoad() restores all keys with correct types and unexpired TTLs
automatic save triggers based on configurable change thresholds (save 900 1)
BGSAVE returns error if already in progress
LASTSAVE returns timestamp of last successful save
corrupted RDB file is detected and rejected on load
binary-safe strings with embedded nulls are serialized correctly
list/set/hash types are serialized and loaded correctly with type preservation

criteria.description: SUBSCRIBE adds client to channel and sends confirmation
criteria.verification: Connect client, run SUBSCRIBE news, verify client in channel->subscribers

criteria.description: PUBLISH delivers message to all channel subscribers
criteria.verification: Subscribe 3 clients, PUBLISH, verify all 3 receive message

criteria.description: PSUBSCRIBE matches channels using glob patterns (*, ?, [])
criteria.verification: PSUBSCRIBE news.*, PUBLISH news.sports, verify message received

criteria.description: Subscribed clients can only run subscription commands and PING
criteria.verification: SUBSCRIBE then GET, verify error returned

criteria.description: Client disconnect removes all subscriptions without memory leak
criteria.verification: Subscribe then kill client, verify no leaked references in server state

criteria.description: Slow subscribers with full buffers are disconnected
criteria.verification: Set small buffer limit, flood with messages, verify client disconnected

criteria.description: PUBSUB CHANNELS returns active channels
criteria.verification: Subscribe to ch1, ch2, PUBSUB CHANNELS returns [ch1, ch2]

criteria.description: PUBSUB NUMSUB returns subscriber counts
criteria.verification: Subscribe 2 clients to ch1, PUBSUB NUMSUB ch1 returns [2]

criteria.criterion: MULTI command puts client in transaction mode
criteria.verification: redis-cli MULTI returns +OK, subsequent commands return +QUEUED

criteria.criterion: Queued commands are not executed until EXEC
criteria.verification: SET in transaction doesn't actually set, must EXEC to see result

criteria.criterion: EXEC returns array of results for each queued command
criteria.verification:
EXEC returns *n
... where n = number of queued commands
MULTI immediately followed by EXEC returns *0
EXEC without MULTI returns -ERR EXEC without MULTI
MULTI then MULTI returns -ERR MULTI calls not nested
DISCARD after queued commands clears queue, GET returns (nil) not old value
WATCH key, change key in another client, EXEC returns nil
WATCH, MULTI, another client modifies key, EXEC returns nil (not array)

criteria.criterion: Error in transaction does not abort remaining commands
criteria.verification: SET on string, LPUSH on same key (error), GET on string returns value - no rollback
WATCH key, UNWATCH, key modified, EXEC executes normally (not nil)
Client A in transaction, Client B commands execute immediately - no interaction

criteria.criterion: CRC16 key-to-slot mapping is deterministic and O(1)
criteria.test: Hash same key multiple times, verify consistent slot
Verify {user}:1 and {user}:2 map to same slot

criteria.criterion: MOVED redirect returned for keys owned by other nodes
criteria.test: Request key owned by other node, verify MOVED response
Start migration, request key, verify ASK response
Meet two nodes, verify each sees other in CLUSTER NODES

criteria.criterion: Gossip failure detection marks unreachable nodes
criteria.test: Disconnect node, wait timeout, verify FAIL status

criteria.criterion: CROSSSLOT error for multi-key ops on different slots
criteria.test: MGET with keys in different slots, verify CROSSSLOT

criteria.criterion: CLUSTER ADDSLOTS assigns slot ownership correctly
criteria.test: Add slots, verify slot_owner array updated

criteria.criterion: Slot migration with ASKING command works
criteria.test: Migrate slot, use ASKING, verify key accessible
Save config, restart, load config, verify cluster state
